cases.doc:
SECURITY_AUDIT C3 \xc2\xb7 a per-charge amount/cap exceeds the wallet's
immutable absolute ceiling (`max_absolute_per_charge`, set once at
`__constructor`). Caps the per-charge drain independent of the ratio
guards, even with a fully compromised admin.
cases.name: ExceedsAbsoluteCeiling

udt_struct_v0.doc: On-chain per-merchant spending policy. The wallet authorizes any\n`token.transfer` whose merchant + amount + interval fall inside these\nconstraints, without consulting the user's passkey again.
udt_struct_v0.name: Policy

fields.doc: Expected charge amount per cycle (informational).
fields.name: amount_per_charge

fields.doc: Unix timestamp after which the policy auto-revokes. 0 = no expiry.
fields.name: expires_at

fields.doc: Minimum gap between successful charges, in seconds.
fields.name: interval_seconds

fields.doc: Ledger timestamp of the most recent authorized charge under this\npolicy. 0 = never charged.
fields.name: last_charge_at

fields.doc: Hard cap per cycle. Charges above this are rejected by `__check_auth`.
fields.name: max_per_charge

fields.doc: Exact merchant Address allowed to pull funds under this policy.
fields.name: merchant

fields.doc: User-set kill switch. Once true, all subsequent merchant pulls fail\nauthorization until the user re-installs the policy.
fields.name: revoked

fields.doc: Token contract (SEP-41) the merchant may transfer from this wallet.
fields.name: token

void_v0.doc: Passkey public key (secp256r1, 65-byte uncompressed X9.62 = 0x04 ||\nX || Y). Set once at deploy via `__constructor` (atomic with deploy \xe2\x80\x94\nSECURITY_AUDIT C2: no un-inited window an observer can front-run).
void_v0.name: PasskeyPubkey

void_v0.doc: Optional passkey credential id (returned by WebAuthn `navigator\n.credentials.create`). Stored so the frontend can issue a correct\n`allowCredentials` parameter on subsequent `get` calls.
void_v0.name: PasskeyCredId

void_v0.doc:
v0.1 administrator. Calls to `install_policy` and `revoke_policy`
require this address's `require_auth`. For the spike, admin is a
classic Ed25519 G-account (the trusted-setup oracle that operates
the spike server). v0.2 changes `init` to set admin =
`env.current_contract_address()` so that install/revoke flow back
through `__check_auth` and require the user's passkey.
void_v0.name: Admin

tuple_v0.doc: Per-merchant policy. Address is keyed verbatim \xe2\x80\x94 a policy applies to\nexactly one merchant address (no wildcards in v0.1).
tuple_v0.name: Policy

tuple_v0.doc: Delegated agent session, keyed by the agent's ed25519 session pubkey.
tuple_v0.name: AgentSession

void_v0.doc:
SECURITY_AUDIT C3 \xc2\xb7 immutable absolute ceiling on any single per-charge
amount/cap, set once at `__constructor` and NEVER settable again. Both
`install_policy` and `install_agent_session` reject any amount/cap above
this value, so a fully compromised admin cannot drain more than this in a
single charge regardless of the ratio guards (N1 / A2.3).
void_v0.name: MaxAbsolutePerCharge

udt_struct_v0.doc: Agent credential carried in the `__check_auth` signature slot: the agent's\ned25519 session pubkey plus its signature over the host payload.
udt_struct_v0.name: AgentAuth

function_v0.doc:
SECURITY_AUDIT C2 \xc2\xb7 guarded no-op kept only to default-deny a stray
`init` call. Initialization is now atomic via `__constructor`, so by the
time the contract exists `PasskeyPubkey` is always present. Any direct
`init` invocation \xe2\x80\x94 e.g. a front-runner attempting the old C2 exploit \xe2\x80\x94
therefore always errors `AlreadyInitialized`. It can never (re)claim
ownership or reset state.
function_v0.name: init

udt_union_v0.doc: The wallet's custom-account signature type (CAP-46-11). The caller declares\nwhich principal is authorizing: the human passkey (real WebAuthn assertion,\nsecp256r1) or a delegated agent session (ed25519).
udt_union_v0.name: WalletAuth

udt_struct_v0.doc:
Delegated agent spending session. Unlike `Policy` (a *pull* grant keyed by
a single merchant), this is a *push* grant: a delegated ed25519 session key
the user authorizes once (via passkey) so an autonomous agent can initiate
transfers within an aggregate budget \xe2\x80\x94 without a fresh human signature per
transaction.

**A3 \xe2\x80\x94 sliding-window counter.** The budget is enforced with an O(1)
sliding-window counter (no per-tx history). We keep the spend of the
current epoch (`cur_spent`) and the immediately preceding epoch
(`prev_spent`), and estimate the rolling spend as a time-weighted sum of
the two for throughput shaping, while staying gas-bounded under
high-frequency agent traffic.

**Worst-case guarantee (N-A3).** The time-weighted estimate counts
`cur_spent` at full weight regardless of *when* in the epoch it was spent.
A "delayed straddle" \xe2\x80\x94 spend late in one epoch, roll, spend early in the
next \xe2\x80\x94 can therefore place spend that exceeds `window_cap` inside a single
real `window_seconds`-length interval (su
udt_struct_v0.name: AgentSession

fields.doc: Recipient allowlist. Empty = open (any recipient within budget). When\nnon-empty, the agent may only pay these addresses \xe2\x80\x94 the core of the\nagent-to-agent guarantee (pay only approved counterparties).
fields.name: allow_recipients

fields.doc: Amount spent so far in the current epoch.
fields.name: cur_spent

fields.doc: Unix timestamp the current epoch opened. Epochs are rolled lazily on\ncharge: a charge at `now >= epoch_start + window_seconds` opens a new\nepoch anchored at `now` (sliding, not grid-aligned).
fields.name: epoch_start

fields.doc: Unix timestamp after which the session auto-revokes. 0 = no expiry.
fields.name: expires_at

fields.doc: Hard cap per single transfer.
fields.name: per_tx_cap

fields.doc: Amount spent in the immediately preceding epoch. Decays linearly across\nthe current epoch in the rolling estimate. 0 if the gap to the previous\nepoch was >= 2 windows (fully decayed).
fields.name: prev_spent

fields.doc: User-set kill switch. Once true, all agent transfers fail until the\nsession is re-installed.
fields.name: revoked

fields.doc: ed25519 public key the agent signs transfer payloads with.
fields.name: session_pubkey

fields.doc:
SSL/Axl provenance: sha256 of the `.ssl` spec that governed this agent
at install time. The contract does not interpret it (SSL is declarative,
not runtime-enforced here) \xe2\x80\x94 it pins it immutably so every spend made
under this session is non-repudiably tied to one governing policy. The
off-chain drift detector diffs observed spend against the spec at this
hash; that diff, not the hash, is the compliance evidence.
fields.name: ssl_hash

fields.doc: Token contract (SEP-41) the agent may transfer from this wallet.
fields.name: token

fields.doc: Aggregate cap across the rolling window. The time-weighted estimate of\nspend across (prev epoch, current epoch) must stay <= this.
fields.name: window_cap

fields.doc: Length of the spending window, in seconds (e.g. 86_400 = 24h).
fields.name: window_seconds

udt_struct_v0.doc:
A REAL WebAuthn assertion (what `navigator.credentials.get()` returns on a
Face ID / fingerprint tap), carried in the `__check_auth` signature slot.

The platform authenticator does NOT sign the raw Soroban payload \xe2\x80\x94 it signs
`SHA256(authenticator_data || SHA256(client_data_json))` with the device's
secp256r1 key, and embeds the challenge (= the Soroban signature_payload,
base64url-no-pad) inside `client_data_json`. `__check_auth` therefore:
1. binds the assertion to THIS transaction \xe2\x80\x94 base64url(signature_payload)
MUST appear in `client_data_json` (replay/cross-tx defense),
2. reconstructs the WebAuthn signing digest and verifies it against the
device's stored secp256r1 pubkey.
`signature` is the raw 64-byte (r||s) form; the frontend converts the
authenticator's DER signature before submitting.
udt_struct_v0.name: WebAuthnAuth

function_v0.doc: Read-only accessor used by the frontend to render the four-guarantee\npanel. Returns the policy as stored or panics with `PolicyNotFound`.
function_v0.name: get_policy

function_v0.doc:
Custom account interface (CAP-46-11). Called by the Soroban host on
every auth attempt where this contract is the authorizing principal.

Authorization rule (SECURITY_AUDIT A1 \xe2\x80\x94 dispatch on credential FIRST,
exactly one authorization model per entry):
- `WalletAuth::Agent`: authenticate the ed25519 session key, then
authorize EVERY context against the session's allowlist + windowed
budget. This path NEVER runs the pull-policy loop, so an agent
credential can never mutate policy `last_charge_at`.
- `WalletAuth::Passkey`: first try the pull-policy path \xe2\x80\x94 if **every**
`auth_context` matches an active on-chain policy (a
`token.transfer(this_wallet, merchant, amount)` with a non-revoked,
non-expired policy, `amount <= max_per_charge`, interval elapsed),
authorize without consulting the signature, bumping `last_charge_at`
per matched policy. Otherwise verify the passkey secp256r1 signature
over `signature_payload` (panics on failure \xe2\x86\x92 host auth rejection).
function_v0.name: __check_auth

function_v0.doc:
SECURITY_AUDIT C2 \xc2\xb7 atomic deploy+init constructor. Soroban runs
`__constructor` exactly once, in the SAME transaction as the deploy that
creates the contract. There is therefore NO un-inited window between
deploy and init for an observer to front-run with their own passkey +
admin (the C2 finding). The wallet is fully owned by the deployer-chosen
principals the instant it exists on-chain.

Args:
- `passkey_pubkey`: the passkey's secp256r1 public key (65-byte
uncompressed X9.62 = 0x04 || X || Y).
- `passkey_cred_id`: the WebAuthn credential id.
- `admin`: gates `install_policy` / `revoke_policy` / agent-session
mutations in v0.1. For the spike, callers pass the deployer's classic
G-account so the trusted-setup server can sign these. v0.2 migrates the
admin to the wallet's own contract address so install/revoke flow back
through `__check_auth` and are gated by the user's passkey.
- `max_absolute_per_charge`: SECURITY_AUDIT C3 \xc2\xb7 the IMMUTABLE absolute
ceiling on any single per-charge amount/cap. Set once here
function_v0.name: __constructor

function_v0.doc: User-controlled kill switch. After this call, all further merchant\npulls fail authorization until `install_policy` is called again with\na fresh passkey signature.
function_v0.name: revoke_policy

function_v0.doc:
Install (or replace) a spending policy for a specific merchant. Requires
the wallet's own auth \xe2\x80\x94 i.e., a passkey signature validated by
`__check_auth`. This is the *only* path to grant a merchant the right
to pull funds.
function_v0.name: install_policy

function_v0.doc: Read-only accessor for a delegated agent session.
function_v0.name: get_agent_session

function_v0.doc: User-controlled kill switch for a delegated agent session. After this\ncall, all agent transfers under this key fail until the session is\nre-installed.
function_v0.name: revoke_agent_session

function_v0.doc: Install (or replace) a delegated agent spending session. Gated by the\nsame admin as `install_policy` in v0.1.
function_v0.name: install_agent_session
